heading: Eligible applicants exempt from asset reporting
label: 1087ss Eligible applicants exempt from asset reporting
notes: References in TextThe Social Security Act, referred to in subsec. (b)(4)(H)(i), (iii), (v), is , . Title XVI of the Act is classified generally to subchapter XVI (§ 1381 et seq.) of chapter 7 of Title 42. L. 109–171, title VIII, § 8018(b)120 Stat. 175Feb. 8, 2006“(1).—Eligibility guidelinesThe Secretary of Education shall regularly evaluate the impact of the eligibility guidelines in subsections (b)(1)(A)(i), (b)(1)(B)(i), (c)(1)(A), and (c)(2)(A) of section 479 of the Higher Education Act of 1965 (, (b)(1)(B)(i), (c)(1)(A), and (c)(2)(A)).20 U.S.C. 1087ss(b)(1)(A)(i)“(2).—Means-tested federal benefit programFor each 3-year period, the Secretary of Education shall evaluate the impact of including the receipt of benefits by a student or parent under a means-tested Federal benefit program (as defined in section 479(d) of the Higher Education Act of 1965 ()[)] as a factor in determining eligibility under subsections (b) and (c) of section 479 of the Higher Education Act of 1965 ( and (c)).”20 U.S.C. 1087ss(d)20 U.S.C. 1087ss(b)

L. 109–171, title VIII, § 8018(b)120 Stat. 175Feb. 8, 2006“(1).—Eligibility guidelinesThe Secretary of Education shall regularly evaluate the impact of the eligibility guidelines in subsections (b)(1)(A)(i), (b)(1)(B)(i), (c)(1)(A), and (c)(2)(A) of section 479 of the Higher Education Act of 1965 (, (b)(1)(B)(i), (c)(1)(A), and (c)(2)(A)).20 U.S.C. 1087ss(b)(1)(A)(i)“(2).—Means-tested federal benefit programFor each 3-year period, the Secretary of Education shall evaluate the impact of including the receipt of benefits by a student or parent under a means-tested Federal benefit program (as defined in section 479(d) of the Higher Education Act of 1965 ()[)] as a factor in determining eligibility under subsections (b) and (c) of section 479 of the Higher Education Act of 1965 ( and (c)).”20 U.S.C. 1087ss(d)20 U.S.C. 1087ss(b)

children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of law, this section shall be effective for each individual seeking to apply for Federal financial aid under this subchapter, as part of the simplified application for Federal student financial aid under , on or after .section 1090 of this titleJuly 1, 2024

children.heading: Applicants exempt from asset reporting
children.label: b Applicants exempt from asset reporting

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Except as provided in paragraph (3), in carrying out , the Secretary shall not use asset information from an eligible applicant or, as applicable, the parent or spouse of an eligible applicant.section 1090 of this title

children.label: A
children.text: Is an applicant who qualifies for an automatic zero student aid index or negative student aid index under subsection (b) or (c) of .section 1087mm of this title

children.label: i
children.text: do not file a Schedule C (or the equivalent successor schedule) with the Federal income tax return for the second preceding tax year; or

children.label: ii
children.text: file a Schedule C (or the equivalent successor schedule) with net business income of not more than a $10,000 loss or gain with the Federal income tax return for the second preceding tax year.

children.label: i
children.text: does not file a Schedule C (or the equivalent successor schedule) with the Federal income tax return for the second preceding tax year; or

children.label: ii
children.text: files a Schedule C (or the equivalent successor schedule) with net business income of not more than a $10,000 loss or gain with the Federal income tax return for the second preceding tax year.

children.label: D
children.text: Is an applicant who, at any time during the previous 24-month period, received a benefit under a means-tested Federal benefit program (or whose parent or spouse received such a benefit, as applicable).

children.label: A
children.text: reside in the United States or a United States territory; or

children.label: B
children.text: file taxes in the United States or a United States territory, except if such nonfiling is due to not being required to file a Federal tax return for the applicable tax year due to a low income.

children.heading: Schedule A
children.label: A Schedule A
children.text: The term “Schedule A” means a form or information by a taxpayer to report itemized deductions.

children.heading: Schedule B
children.label: B Schedule B
children.text: The term “Schedule B” means a form or information filed by a taxpayer to report interest and ordinary dividend income.

children.heading: Schedule C
children.label: C Schedule C
children.text: The term “Schedule C” means a form or information filed by a taxpayer to report income or loss from a business operated or a profession practiced as a sole proprietor.

children.heading: Schedule D
children.label: D Schedule D
children.text: The term “Schedule D” means a form or information filed by a taxpayer to report sales, exchanges or some involuntary conversions of capital assets, certain capital gain distributions, and nonbusiness bad debts.

children.heading: Schedule E
children.label: E Schedule E
children.text: The term “Schedule E” means a form or information filed by a taxpayer to report income from rental properties, royalties, partnerships, S corporations, estates, trusts, and residual interests in real estate mortgage investment conduits.

children.heading: Schedule F
children.label: F Schedule F
children.text: The term “Schedule F” means a form or information filed by a taxpayer to report farm income and expenses.

children.heading: Schedule H
children.label: G Schedule H
children.text: The term “Schedule H” means a form or information filed by a taxpayer to report household employment taxes.

children.label: i
children.text: The supplemental security income program under title XVI of the Social Security Act ( et seq.).42 U.S.C. 1381

children.label: ii
children.text: The supplemental nutrition assistance program under the Food and Nutrition Act of 2008 ( et seq.), a nutrition assistance program carried out under section 19 of such Act (), and a supplemental nutrition assistance program carried out under .7 U.S.C. 20117 U.S.C. 2028section 1841(c) of title 48

children.label: iii
children.text: The program of block grants for States for temporary assistance for needy families established under part A of title IV of the Social Security Act ( et seq.).42 U.S.C. 601

children.label: iv
children.text: The special supplemental nutrition program for women, infants, and children established by .section 1786 of title 42

children.label: v
children.text: The Medicaid program under title XIX of the Social Security Act ( et seq.).42 U.S.C. 1396

children.label: vi
children.text: Federal housing assistance programs, including tenant-based assistance under section 1437f() of title 42, and public housing, as defined in .osection 1437a(b)(1) of title 42

children.label: vii
children.text: Other means-tested programs determined by the Secretary to be approximately consistent with the income eligibility requirements of the means-tested programs under clauses (i) through (vi).
